proposed_base_material_variables.alloy_family_classification: A specific subgrouping or family designation of the base material based on distinct chemical characteristics (e.g., high nitrogen, high silicon, low manganese) that correlates to localized corrosion resistance.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.specimen_geometry_and_orientation: The physical dimensions, shape, and geometric configuration of the tested specimen, including whether the exposed test surface is parallel to the rolling plane, which can influence localized corrosion behavior.

proposed_experiment_variables.critical_pitting_temperature_CPT: The critical temperature below which pit initiation does not occur on a flat surface in a standardized oxidizing chloride solution.
proposed_experiment_variables.crevice_corrosion_temperature_CCT: The minimum temperature at which crevice corrosion initiates under a specific artificial crevice configuration in a standardized testing solution.
proposed_experiment_variables.electrolyte_composition: The precise chemical makeup and concentration of the liquid testing medium (e.g., 10% FeCl3, 37.5% HCl).
proposed_experiment_variables.crevice_former_type: The specific materials and mechanical setups used to simulate occluded cell geometries and built-in crevices (e.g., Teflon-to-metal, rubber band-to-metal).
proposed_experiment_variables.exposure_duration: The precise length of time the specimen is continuously exposed to the testing environment at a given test parameter or temperature interval.
proposed_experiment_variables.aeration_or_purging_gas: The specific environmental gas condition of the electrolyte, such as nitrogen purging or naturally aerated, which controls the dissolved oxygen content available for cathodic reactions.
proposed_experiment_variables.measured_corrosion_rate: The quantitative rate of active corrosion determined during the test, typically calculated via measured weight loss and expressed in specific units such as inches per month (ipm) or mm/year.
